title: Welcome to FIGN · how the federation works
hook: What FIGN is, how the federation works, and why your org's logo stays on everything it makes.
body: FIGN is an umbrella. Femmes aux Consoles, Bambina, Nexal, and others are peers under it. Every event, workshop, and lesson carries its creator's brand.

title: Your map · how interests work
hook: Your map isn't a career track. It's what you love, plus what you've done.
body: There are no stages. There are no tracks. You pick interests, write a sentence if you want, and your map starts there.

title: Welcoming newcomers · the first 10 minutes
hook: What to say when a woman opens FIGN for the first time.
body: Three sentences. A question. A handoff to someone she can squad with. That's the welcome.

title: Hosting a play-night · the 2-hour run sheet
hook: By the end: a run sheet, a welcome script, a safety checklist.
body: Two hours is enough. Four blocks of 25 minutes, two breaks, one closing circle.

title: Finding a mentor · writing the first note
hook: Three sentences that make her say yes.
body: Be specific. Name the thing you want. Offer something back. End with a real question.

title: Digital safety · the essentials
hook: Your handle, your DMs, your recordings — choose before someone chooses for you.
body: Five rules you can follow tonight.

title: Moderation for stream chats
hook: The three chat-mod rules every woman streamer uses.
body: Three rules, three hotkeys, one trusted mod.

title: Harassment · the escalation path
hook: From screenshots to platform reports to allies — a path, not a flowchart.
body: Know who to tell, and what to keep.

title: Reading MK1 frame data under pressure
hook: Drill the frames until they feel like muscle memory.
body: Four common blockstrings, punished in real-time.

title: Punish optimisation · MK1
hook: The three punishes you'll quietly regret not learning early.
body: D2, high-low mixup, corner carry.

title: Mental game · losing without tilting
hook: Lose on purpose. Then lose for real. Then keep playing.
body: A short protocol for resetting after a bad set.

title: Voice acting for games · intro
hook: Cold-read a real script from Vodou: A Space Odyssey.
body: A 45-word scene. Your warm-up. Your read. Your second read.

title: Cold-reading a script · practice
hook: The drill that makes cold-reads feel like warm-reads.
body: Three scripts. Three takes each. No re-recording between the first and second.

title: Cosplay sewing · basic stitches
hook: Running stitch, backstitch, whipstitch — the three that build a wardrobe.
body: By the end: a finished hem, a lined seam, and the confidence to cut fabric you can't replace.

title: Foam armour · starter pattern
hook: A pauldron, a forearm, a greave. Enough to cosplay a guardian.
body: EVA foam, Barge cement, a heat gun. That's it.

title: Shoutcasting 101
hook: The three sentences every caster masters first.
body: Setup. Stakes. Surprise.

title: OBS for new streamers
hook: From install to first 'Go Live'.
body: A scene for desktop, a scene for game, a scene for your face.

title: Unity · your first scene
hook: Install to a scene with a moving cube in under 35 minutes.
body: Unity Hub, a new 2D project, a script, a build.

title: Godot · build a 2D platformer
hook: A playable platformer in under an hour.
body: Scene tree, gravity, jump, collect, win screen.

title: Narrative design · the shape of a beat
hook: One beat, three reveals, one reversal. The shape that makes a story feel alive.
body: Apply this to a Juju Games scene you've never seen before.
